content.content:
content: "# Web Document

## Metadata

- title: myHealth Online | Santa Clara\
  \ Valley Medical Center
- url: https://scvmc.scvh.org/patients-visitors/patient-services/myhealth-online
\
  - source: Santa Clara Valley Healthcare
- domain: scvmc.scvh.org

## Content
\
  
## About myHealth Online

myHealth Online is an easy-to-use secure web site\
  \ that gives you access to your health information. It is a customized version of\
  \ MyChart. The myHealth Online website and MyChart mobile app allow you to view\
  \ your test results, request prescription refills, make an appointment, and message\
  \ your doctor directly from your computer or smartphone.

Patients may use the\
  \ Log In, Sign Up Now, and Download App links to access myHealth Online or the MyChart\
  \ mobile app.

## Special Features

Santa Clara Valley Medical Center’\
  s myHealth Online makes managing your health easier than ever with secure access\
  \ anytime, anywhere. The page lists special features including:

- View Test Result
\
  - Ask a Question
- Schedule an Appointment
- Request Health Records
- Link your\
  \ Account
- Refill Medications
- Review your Health Summary
- View Billing Summary
\
  - Share Everywhere
- Manage your Notifications

## Secure Online Bill Pay

\
  Patients can see statements and pay bills online. A myHealth Online account is not\
  \ required to use the online bill pay service. For questions, patients may call\
  \ the 24/7 myHealth Online Help Desk at 408-977-3524.

## eCheck-In

Patients\
  \ can save time using eCheck-In prior to an appointment. The page links to an eCheck-In\
  \ information sheet.

## myHealth Online Support Options

Support options include\
  \ a step-by-step user guide with screenshots and directions for myHealth Online\
  \ features, frequently asked questions based on common patient feedback, and help\
  \ for account or feature issues.
"

content: "# Web Document

## Metadata

- title: Requesting Medical Records | O'Connor\
  \ Hospital
- url: https://och.scvh.org/patients-visitors/services/requesting-medical-records
\
  - source: O'Connor Hospital, Santa Clara Valley Healthcare
- domain: och.scvh.org
\
  
## Content

# Requesting Medical Records

## How to request a copy of your\
  \ medical records or x-ray and radiology images

### I. Complete a Release of\
  \ Information Form

Patients or their legal representatives can get copies of\
  \ their medical records, or have copies sent to another facility. To do this, O’\
  Connor Hospital requires a completed and signed form before it can release the records\
  \ to anyone, including the patient.

Patients should print and complete the Request\
  \ for Release of Medical Record Information form. The form must be completed, dated,\
  \ and signed. The page provides release forms in multiple languages, including English,\
  \ Spanish, Vietnamese, Farsi, Simplified Chinese, Traditional Chinese, Tagalog,\
  \ Russian, and Punjabi.

Patients should specify which parts of the medical records\
  \ they wish to obtain. The page notes that the discharge summary, operative report,\
  \ and history and physical exam often contain information needed for continued care.
\
  
Requests or authorizations for certain information must be initiated as indicated\
  \ on the form by the authorized person, including:

- Psychiatric Care
- AIDS/HIV
\
  - Alcohol/drug abuse

For questions about the release of medical record information,\
  \ patients may call (408) 947-4719. For radiology films or the CD library, patients\
  \ may call (408) 947-4730.

### II. Submitting Your Request

After completing\
  \ the request for release of medical information, patients can submit the request\
  \ by mail, in person to the Health Information Management department, or by faxing\
  \ the form.

Mail or in person:

O’Connor Hospital  
Health Information\
  \ Management Department  
Release of Information  
2105 Forest Avenue  
San Jose,\
  \ CA 95128

The Health Information Management Services (Medical Records) Department\
  \ is located on the first floor of O’Connor Hospital, across from the Gift\
  \ Shop.

Fax:

(408) 947-3434  
Title Attn.: Release of Information

Hours:
\
  
Monday to Friday, 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. Closed weekends and holidays.

If the department\
  \ is closed, there is a specific mailbox where patients can drop off the request\
  \ by the Medical Record Department door.

### Additional Information

If someone\
  \ else will pick up the records, the representative must have an original, signed\
  \ authorization letter from the patient and a photo ID.

Patients should allow\
  \ up to 10 business days or 14 calendar days for the request to be processed. If\
  \ the patient asks to pick up forms in person, the Release of Information Office\
  \ will contact the patient when records are ready. A photo ID is required.

##\
  \ Cost for Copy of Information

Records requested for a physician or other provider\
  \ are sent to the provider at no cost. If the patient wants a copy for personal\
  \ records, costs may apply, including $6.50 for the electronic portion of the record\
  \ and $0.07 per page for paper portions. If charges exceed $100, payment may be\
  \ required before release. For x-ray or radiology images, the first CD or set of\
  \ films is free; additional copies cost $15 for a CD or $15 for each film.
"
metadata: {}
title: Requesting Medical Records | O'Connor Hospital
url: https://och.scvh.org/patients-visitors/services/requesting-medical-records

content: "# Web Document

## Metadata

- title: Release of Medical Records | MediCopy
\
  - url: https://medicopy.net/roi
- source: MediCopy
- domain: medicopy.net

\
  ## Content

# Release of Medical Records

MediCopy can only release medical\
  \ records from healthcare entities who have partnered with MediCopy for release\
  \ of information.

If a healthcare provider did not direct the patient to MediCopy\
  \ to obtain medical records, the patient should contact the provider directly. If\
  \ the healthcare provider did direct the patient to MediCopy to obtain medical records,\
  \ the patient may proceed to request records.

## Steps For Requesting Your Medical\
  \ Records

### Step 1

Submit an authorization form via Web or PDF.

Option\
  \ 1: Submit online

- Online Authorization Form (English)
- Online Authorization\
  \ Form (Espa\xF1ol)

Option 2: Download and submit online, by mail, or in person
\
  
- PDF Authorization Form (English)
- PDF Authorization Form (Espa\xF1ol)

\
  Online submission is available through an upload link. Patients may also mail forms\
  \ to MediCopy at 8 City Blvd. Suite 400, Nashville, TN 37209, or drop the completed\
  \ form off at the doctor’s office.

### Step 2

Once received, MediCopy\
  \ begins fulfilling the request. The authorization should be completed in its entirety.\
  \ Missing required information can delay fulfillment.

### Step 3

Records will\
  \ be sent by the delivery method specified on the authorization form.

## Patient\
  \ Resources and Contact

The page provides resources such as Frequently Asked\
  \ Questions, online bill payment, and contact links. MediCopy contact information\
  \ is listed as:

MediCopy  
8 City Blvd. Suite 400  
Nashville, TN 37209  
\
  Office: (866) 587-6274
"

content.content:
Use **O’Connor Hospital’s formal medical-record release process**, rather than MediCopy or the portal alone.

### Why
- **myHealth Online** can share portal-visible information, such as visit summaries, test results, and health summaries.
- Your new doctor, **Bay Area Orthopedics**, requires:
  - Recent visit records
  - History and physical
  - Discharge summary
  - Radiology report or images
- Because this includes records beyond the portal and **radiology materials**, O’Connor’s Health Information Management release process is the appropriate option.
- **MediCopy** should be used only if O’Connor specifically directed you there; the hospital’s information does not indicate that it did.

### Next step
A release authorization draft has been prepared but **not submitted**. Complete, date, and sign O’Connor’s Request for Release of Medical Record Information form, specifying Bay Area Orthopedics as the recipient and listing the records above.

Submit it to:

**O’Connor Hospital**  
Health Information Management Department — Release of Information  
2105 Forest Avenue  
San Jose, CA 95128  

Or fax it to **(408) 947-3434**, Attn: Release of Information.

For questions, call **(408) 947-4719**. For radiology images specifically, call **(408) 947-4730**.

O’Connor says provider-directed records are sent at no cost and requests may take up to **10 business days**.
